changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6 — work_id: BE-W1-PR5, branch: feature/reference-type-modelling-and-review-ux-completion, risk_level: STANDARD, execution_model: TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type: MIXED. All five mandatory fields present and correct.

backend/core/llm/prompts/parsing_prompt_pdf.txt:6-27 — Schema fields: id, name, value, unit, reference, raw_reference_text, context_range_options, confidence, ref_low, ref_high. No referenceType field. No labelled_bands array. context_range_options is live from PR4 (lines 14-22). Sprint must add referenceType to schema and a structured labelled_bands block.

backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:34-53 — ParsedBiomarker Pydantic model fields: id, name, value, unit, reference, confidence, ref_low, ref_high, status, raw_reference_text, context_range_options. No referenceType or labelled_bands field. Output dict lines 435-459 emits contextRangeOptions but no referenceType. Sprint must add model field and output dict key.

backend/app/routes/upload.py:124-149 — Pure pass-through enrichment with SSOT metadata. No Intelligence Core interaction. No scoring logic. New parser fields will flow through automatically once added to parser output dict. No HIGH-risk path touched.

frontend/app/types/parsed.ts:15-39 — ParsedBiomarker interface fields: name, value, unit, status, referenceRange, referenceText, contextRangeOptions. No referenceType, no labelledBands. ContextRangeOption interface lines 6-13 exists and is live. Sprint must extend ParsedBiomarker with referenceType union type and labelled-bands structure.

frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:15-17 — RangeAttention type: 'none' | 'one-sided' | 'partial' | 'missing' | 'context-selection-required'. Missing new states for labelled-bands-resolved, no-lab-range-supplied, incomplete-or-ambiguous. rangeAttentionLevel lines 174-192: not type-driven. buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow lines 78-169: no referenceType extraction. CRITICAL: referenceRangeToPayload() lines 197-210 MUST remain unchanged — payload contract safe.

frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx:226-239 — Biomarker transformer builds referenceRange+referenceText+contextRangeOptions. Must also pass through referenceType and labelledBands when present. Attention alert guard lines 356-364 checks att !== 'none' && att !== 'one-sided'; new attention states will be caught automatically once RangeAttention is extended.

frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx:33-81 — rangeBadge() handles context-selection-required, missing, one-sided, partial, none. Missing badge variants for labelled-bands-resolved, no-lab-range-supplied, incomplete-or-ambiguous. Sprint must add these badge cases.

frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx:386-411 — refMin/refMax inputs are plain type=number — no comparator selector. Context band selection lines 337-372 is live from PR4, driven by biomarker.contextRangeOptions?.length > 1 — must not regress. No labelled-bands display section. No no-range-supplied neutral state. Sprint must add comparator selector (narrowly scoped to one-sided mode), labelled-bands display, and no-range-supplied state.

frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:197-210 (referenceRangeToPayload) is the single payload contract and MUST remain unchanged. referenceType is declared review-stage modelling only (prompt Arch Decision 1-2). No second scoring authority created.

changes.check: 2A — Forbidden HIGH-risk files not in scope
changes.citation: Files in scope: backend/core/llm/prompts/parsing_prompt_pdf.txt, backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py, backend/app/routes/upload.py, frontend/app/types/parsed.ts, frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts, frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx, frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx, frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx. NOT in scope: backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py.

Prompt §Scope lines 235-335: bounded to PDF prompt/schema extension, parser model, upload route passthrough, TS contract types, rangeAttentionLevel, ParsedTable badges, EditDialog comparator + labelled-bands + no-range-supplied, page transformer passthrough. Non-goals (lines 339-348): no scoring policy, no fallback ranges, no second authority, no results/narrative, no value-level inequality parsing, no broad parser redesign.

Prompt §Regression Targets lines 397-432: parse extraction (raw text preserved, referenceType emitted, labelled_bands and context_range_options returned), review/edit behaviour (type-driven, auto-resolve labelled bands, applicability bands require confirmation, comparator editing works, no-range-supplied distinct valid state), analysis handoff (referenceRange contract unchanged, no scoring changes), PR4 non-regression (applicability-band selection cases still work).

STOP1: no scoring change required. STOP2: LLM can emit referenceType stably as a new JSON field; no broad parser rewrite needed. STOP3: referenceType is review-stage modelling, not second range authority. STOP4: referenceRangeToPayload() unchanged. STOP5: applicability bands preserve user confirmation per prompt Arch Decision 4. STOP6: no_lab_range_supplied emittable conservatively per prompt instructions. STOP7: value-level inequality explicitly out of scope (prompt lines 225-229). STOP8: changes are bounded additions to specific seam files, not broad flow redesign.

Risk 1: LLM non-determinism for referenceType is accepted; mitigated by conservative emission rules in prompt. Risk 2: labelled-band auto-resolution from measured value must be deterministic — same parsed value + same bands = same matched band; no LLM call at resolve time. Risk 3: applicability-band selection requires user confirmation — no silent auto-selection; determinism preserved. Risk 4: buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow passthrough for referenceType and labelledBands must be additive and must not alter existing ref_low/ref_high path for simple markers (lines 78-169). Risk 5: PR4 context-selection-required path (uploadReferenceRange.ts:181-183) must remain intact alongside new type-driven logic.
No root cause compilers, signal evaluators, InsightGraph construction, analytical pipeline logic, or output assembly mechanisms in scope. STANDARD risk level is correct. No risk upgrade required.

All gaps verified by reading actual source content: no referenceType in prompt schema (parsing_prompt_pdf.txt:6-27), no referenceType in ParsedBiomarker model (llm_parser.py:34-53), no referenceType in output dict (llm_parser.py:435-459), no referenceType in TS interface (parsed.ts:15-39), no new RangeAttention states (uploadReferenceRange.ts:15-17), no type-driven attention logic (uploadReferenceRange.ts:174-192), no new badge cases (ParsedTable.tsx:33-81), no comparator selector or labelled-bands UI (EditDialog.tsx:386-411).
